text: Marathon performance time in recreational male runners can be predicted (r=0.81) using mean weekly training distance, mean training pace, and body fat percentage.
Body fat percentage only significantly affects marathon performance when it exceeds a certain threshold; at low body fat levels, performance correlates only with training indices.
Marathon race time (minutes) can be estimated as: 11.03 + 98.46 × exp(-0.0053 × weekly distance [km/week]) + 0.387 × training pace (sec/km) + 0.1 × exp(0.23 × body fat %).
Mean weekly training distance and mean running speed during the training period are significant predictors of marathon race time in recreational runners.
The Tanda marathon prediction equation is valid for finish times in the range of 165-266 minutes (approximately 2:45 to 4:26).
